question_text: A car is travelling at a constant speed in a straight line on a flat road. What can you conclude about the forces acting on the car?

options.label: A
options.text: The forces are unbalanced and the car is accelerating

options.label: B
options.text: The driving force is much larger than friction

options.label: C
options.text: The forces are balanced and the resultant force is zero

options.label: D
options.text: There is no friction acting on the car

explanation: When a car moves at constant speed (not speeding up or slowing down), the driving force must be balanced by friction and air resistance. A zero resultant force means the car continues at the same speed, which is what Newton's first law tells us.
